about.title: About Achieve Pack | Sustainable Packaging Since 2011
about.description: Learn about Achieve Pack's mission to make sustainable packaging accessible. Founded in 2011, we serve 500+ brands with eco-friendly flexible packaging solutions.
about.heroSubtitle: Making sustainable packaging the easy choice since 2011. From Hong Kong to the world, we're helping brands reduce their environmental impact without compromising on quality.
about.introSummary: Achieve Pack is the parent company behind pouch.eco, founded in 2011 with a mission to make sustainable packaging accessible to businesses of all sizes. We combine decades of packaging expertise with a genuine commitment to environmental responsibility.

story.title: Our Story
story.p1: Founded in 2011 in Hong Kong, Achieve Pack® has grown from a small packaging supplier into a leading provider of sustainable flexible packaging solutions. Our journey began with a simple belief: businesses shouldn't have to choose between quality packaging and environmental responsibility.
story.p2: Today, we serve over 500 brands worldwide, from artisan startups to established enterprises, helping them transition to eco-friendly packaging without compromising on quality, performance, or budget.
story.p3: Under the pouch.eco brand, we've made sustainable packaging accessible to businesses of all sizes, with low minimum order quantities and fast turnaround times that traditional manufacturers simply can't match.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.intro: Our mission is to make sustainable packaging the easy choice for every business. We believe that environmental responsibility shouldn't be a premium feature—it should be the standard.
points: Reducing plastic waste in the packaging industry
Making certified eco materials accessible to all business sizes
Helping brands tell their sustainability story
Innovating new materials and solutions for a circular economy

sustainability.title: Sustainability First
sustainability.desc: Every decision we make considers environmental impact. From material selection to shipping methods, sustainability guides our operations.

quality.title: Quality Without Compromise
quality.desc: Eco-friendly doesn't mean lower quality. Our packaging meets the same rigorous standards as conventional materials.

partnership.title: Customer Partnership
partnership.desc: We work as partners, not just suppliers. Your success is our success, and we're invested in helping you achieve your goals.

innovation.title: Innovation & Transparency
innovation.desc: We continuously develop new materials and share honest information about capabilities and limitations.

team.title: Our Team
team.intro: Our team combines decades of packaging industry experience with a passion for sustainability. From material scientists to customer support specialists, every team member is committed to your success.

impact.title: Our Impact
impact.outro: Every pouch we produce represents a step away from conventional plastics and toward a more sustainable future. Join the hundreds of brands already making a difference.

faq.q1: Where is Achieve Pack located?
faq.a1: Our headquarters is in Hong Kong, with manufacturing facilities in China. We ship worldwide and have customers across North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia.
faq.q2: What makes Achieve Pack different from other packaging suppliers?
faq.a2: We specialize exclusively in sustainable packaging with low MOQs (100 pieces), fast turnaround (2-3 weeks for samples), and comprehensive certification documentation. We're partners, not just suppliers.
faq.q3: Do you work with startups and small businesses?
faq.a3: Absolutely! Our low minimum order quantities make sustainable packaging accessible to businesses of all sizes. Many of our customers started with us as startups and have grown with our support.
faq.q4: What certifications do your materials have?
faq.a4: Our materials are certified to various international standards including EN 13432, ASTM D6400, GRS (Global Recycled Standard), and food safety certifications (FDA, EU 10/2011). We provide documentation with every order.

cta.title: Partner With Us
cta.description: Join 500+ brands already making a sustainable impact with Achieve Pack.

certificates.title: Packaging Certifications | EN 13432, GRS, FDA Compliance
certificates.description: Explore Achieve Pack's comprehensive certifications: EN 13432, ASTM D6400, GRS, FDA, BRC, and more. Full documentation provided with every order.
certificates.heroSubtitle: Third-party verified certifications you can trust. From compostability to food safety, our certifications give you confidence in every claim.
certificates.introSummary: Our packaging materials are certified to the world's leading standards for compostability, recyclability, and food safety. We provide complete documentation with every order to support your compliance and marketing needs.

overview.title: Our Certifications & Compliance
overview.p1: At Achieve Pack, we believe that certifications are more than just badges—they're proof of our commitment to quality, safety, and sustainability. Every certification we hold represents rigorous third-party testing and ongoing audits.
overview.p2: We provide full documentation with every order, giving you the confidence to make claims about your packaging and satisfy retailer requirements.

en13432.name: EN 13432
en13432.desc: The European standard for compostable packaging. Requires 90% disintegration within 12 weeks and complete biodegradation within 6 months in industrial composting conditions.

astm.name: ASTM D6400
astm.desc: US standard for plastics designed for municipal/industrial aerobic composting facilities. Testing equivalent to EN 13432, recognized across North America.

australian.name: AS 4736 / AS 5810
australian.desc: AS 4736 for commercial compostability and AS 5810 for home compostability. Required for compostable claims in Australia and New Zealand.

okcompost.name: OK Compost HOME
okcompost.desc: Certification for materials that biodegrade in home composting conditions (20-30°C). The gold standard for consumer-level compostability.

grs.name: GRS - Global Recycled Standard
grs.desc: Our PCR (post-consumer recycled) materials are GRS certified, verifying the recycled content and tracking it through the supply chain. This certification is increasingly required by major retailers.

mono.title: Mono-Material Recyclability
mono.desc: Our mono-PE and mono-PP pouches are designed for recyclability in existing recycling streams. Structure verified by third-party testing.

storeDropOff.title: Store Drop-Off Compatible
storeDropOff.desc: Many of our recyclable pouches qualify for store drop-off recycling programs (like How2Recycle's Store Drop-Off label).

foodSafety.title: Food Safety Certifications
foodSafety.intro: All our packaging materials intended for food contact are certified to international food safety standards. We can provide documentation for regulatory compliance in your target markets.

fda.name: FDA 21 CFR
fda.desc: US Food & Drug Administration compliance for food contact materials

eu.name: EU 10/2011
eu.desc: European regulation for plastic materials in food contact

migration.name: Migration Testing
migration.desc: Third-party verified migration testing for specific food types

iso.name: ISO 9001:2015
iso.desc: Quality Management System certification. Demonstrates our commitment to consistent quality and continuous improvement in all processes.

brc.name: BRC Packaging Materials
brc.desc: British Retail Consortium certification for packaging manufacturers. Often required by major retailers for food packaging suppliers.

fsc.name: FSC Chain of Custody
fsc.desc: For paper-based products, our FSC certification ensures responsibly sourced paper from well-managed forests.

sedex.name: SEDEX Membership
sedex.desc: Ethical trade audit platform. We maintain visibility into our ethical, labor, and environmental practices.

documentation.title: Documentation We Provide
documentation.intro: We can provide the certification documents you need for retailer compliance, regulatory submissions, or marketing claims:

faq.q1: Do you provide certification documents with orders?
faq.a1: Yes, we provide all relevant certification documents with every order. This includes certificates of compliance, food safety declarations, and specific certifications like EN 13432 or GRS certificates as applicable.
faq.q2: Are your compostable materials certified in my country?
faq.a2: Our compostable materials are certified to EN 13432 (EU), ASTM D6400 (US), and AS 4736/5810 (Australia). These are the major international standards. Contact us for specific regional requirements.
faq.q3: Can I use your certifications in my marketing?
faq.a3: Yes, with the proper documentation we provide, you can make certified claims about your packaging. We'll advise on correct wording and logo usage to ensure compliance.
faq.q4: How often are your certifications renewed?
faq.a4: Most certifications require annual audits and renewal. We maintain current certifications and can provide up-to-date documentation at any time.

cta.title: Get Certified Packaging
cta.description: Order eco-friendly packaging with full certification documentation.

factoryTour.title: Virtual Factory Tour | Achieve Pack Manufacturing Facility
factoryTour.description: Take a virtual tour of Achieve Pack's state-of-the-art sustainable packaging facility. See how we manufacture eco-friendly pouches with precision and quality control.
factoryTour.heroSubtitle: Step inside our manufacturing facility where sustainable packaging innovation meets precision engineering. Discover how we bring your eco-friendly pouches to life.
factoryTour.introSummary: Our factory combines modern manufacturing technology with rigorous quality control and sustainable practices. From material sourcing to final inspection, every step is designed to deliver premium eco-friendly packaging.

entrance.title: Factory Entrance
entrance.desc: Welcome to our state-of-the-art manufacturing facility

digital.title: Digital Printing
digital.desc: High-precision digital printing with unlimited colors

roto.title: Rotogravure Printing
roto.desc: Large-scale plate printing for high-volume orders

laminating.title: Laminating Process
laminating.desc: Bonding multiple material layers for barrier protection

slitting.title: Slitting Machine
slitting.desc: Precision cutting of laminated films to required widths

bagMaking.title: Bag Making
bagMaking.desc: Converting films into finished pouches and bags

welcome.title: Welcome to Our Virtual Factory Tour
welcome.p1: Step inside our state-of-the-art manufacturing facility where sustainable packaging comes to life. Our factory combines cutting-edge technology with rigorous quality control to produce eco-friendly pouches that meet the highest international standards.
welcome.p2: While we can't bring you physically to our facility, this virtual tour gives you insight into how we manufacture your packaging with precision, care, and environmental responsibility.

materials.title: Material Sourcing & Storage
points: All raw materials sourced from certified suppliers
Regular quality testing of incoming materials
materials.outro: We maintain partnerships with leading sustainable material manufacturers worldwide, including suppliers of certified compostable films, GRS-certified recycled plastics, and food-safe bio-based materials.

printing.title: Printing Department
printing.intro: Our printing department features both digital and flexographic (plate) printing capabilities, allowing us to handle orders from 100 pieces to millions with consistent quality.

digital.title: Digital Printing
points: Unlimited colors at no extra cost

printing.outro: All inks used are food-safe and compliant with FDA and EU regulations. We use solvent-free and water-based inks where possible.

lamination.title: Lamination & Material Processing
lamination.intro: Our lamination department bonds multiple material layers to create the barrier properties your product needs. This is where we combine materials like kraft paper with PLA, or create mono-material structures for recyclability.

pouchMaking.title: Pouch Making & Conversion
pouchMaking.intro: Our pouch-making lines convert printed and laminated films into finished pouches. We can produce all major pouch formats including stand-up pouches, flat bottom bags, side gusset bags, and more.
pouchMaking.outro: Each line is equipped for different pouch styles and can integrate features like zippers, valves, spouts, and tear notches.

quality.title: Quality Control Laboratory
quality.intro: Our in-house laboratory performs rigorous testing at every production stage. From incoming material inspection to final product testing, we ensure every pouch meets specifications.

sustainability.title: Sustainability Practices
sustainability.intro: Sustainability isn't just in our products—it's in our operations. Our factory implements comprehensive environmental management practices.

waste.title: Waste Reduction
points: Optimized cutting patterns minimize trim waste

faq.q1: Can I visit your factory in person?
faq.a1: Yes, we welcome factory visits by appointment. Contact us to schedule a visit to our manufacturing facility. We also offer detailed video tours for customers who cannot travel.
faq.q2: What quality certifications does your factory have?
faq.a2: Our facility is ISO 9001 certified for quality management, BRC certified for food packaging, and maintains FSC chain of custody certification for paper-based products.
faq.q3: What is your production capacity?
faq.a3: We can produce over 100,000 pouches per day across our production lines. For large orders, we can scale up with advance notice. Rush orders are also possible for urgent needs.
faq.q4: How do you ensure consistency across batches?
faq.a4: We maintain detailed production records, use calibrated equipment, and perform statistical process control (SPC) to ensure every batch meets the same standards. Color matching is verified with spectrophotometers.

cta.title: See It For Yourself
cta.description: Request a personalized video tour or schedule an in-person visit to our facility.

faqs.title: Frequently Asked Questions | Packaging FAQs
faqs.description: Find answers to common questions about sustainable packaging: MOQs, materials, production times, shipping, and more. Comprehensive FAQ from Achieve Pack.
faqs.heroSubtitle: Answers to your packaging questions. Find information about ordering, materials, production, shipping, and more.
faqs.introSummary: Browse our comprehensive FAQ covering ordering, materials, production, shipping, and customization. Can't find your answer? Contact us directly—we're happy to help.

faq.q1: How do I get started?
faq.a1: Simply contact us through our website, email, or WhatsApp. Share your product details and requirements, and we'll provide a quotation within 24-48 hours.
faq.q2: Can I see samples before ordering?
faq.a2: Absolutely! We provide material samples, blank pouches for test filling, and can produce printed samples with your design before full production.
faq.q3: Do you work with small businesses and startups?
faq.a3: Yes! Our low MOQs (100 pieces for digital printing) make us ideal for small businesses. Many of our customers started small and have grown with us.
faq.q4: What support do you provide after delivery?
faq.a4: We provide ongoing support including reorder assistance, design updates, and help resolving any issues. We're partners for the long term, not just one order.

cta.title: Still Have Questions?
cta.description: Our team is ready to help with any questions not covered here.

leadTime.title: Lead Time Guide | Production & Shipping Timeline
leadTime.description: Understand Achieve Pack's lead times for sustainable packaging. Production timelines, shipping options, and tips for planning your packaging orders.
leadTime.heroSubtitle: Plan your packaging orders with confidence. Understand production and shipping timelines for smooth inventory management.
leadTime.introSummary: Total lead time ranges from 3-4 weeks (rush digital + express) to 10-12 weeks (plate printing + sea freight). This guide helps you understand timelines and plan effectively.

faq.q1: Can you guarantee delivery dates?
faq.a1: We provide estimated timelines based on typical production and shipping. While we strive to meet all dates, factors like customs delays or shipping disruptions can occasionally affect delivery. We keep you updated throughout.
faq.q2: What happens if I need to change my delivery date?
faq.a2: Contact us as early as possible. Before production starts, we can usually adjust. Once production begins, changes may not be possible, but we can sometimes adjust shipping speed.
faq.q3: Do you offer rush production?
faq.a3: Yes, for digital printing orders. Rush fees apply and depend on current production load. Contact us with your deadline and we'll confirm if rush is possible.
faq.q4: How do holidays affect lead time?
faq.a4: Chinese New Year (late Jan/Feb) significantly impacts production—plan 2-3 weeks extra. Other holidays have minimal impact. We'll advise on any timing concerns during quotation.

cta.title: Need a Timeline Estimate?
cta.description: Tell us about your order and we'll provide a specific timeline.

highBarrier.title: High Barrier Packaging | 12-24 Month Shelf Life Premium Pouches
highBarrier.description: High barrier eco-friendly packaging for products with 12-24 month shelf life. Recyclable mono-PE with EVOH, mono-PP options. Perfect for coffee, supplements, baby food, and sensitive products.
highBarrier.heroSubtitle: Maximum protection with 12-24 month shelf life. Recyclable high barrier options for premium products.
highBarrier.introSummary: High barrier packaging provides maximum oxygen and moisture protection for products requiring 12-24 month shelf life. Our recyclable mono-PE with EVOH structures offer premium protection while maintaining sustainability credentials.

faq.q1: Can high barrier packaging still be recyclable?
faq.a1: Yes! Our mono-PE with EVOH layer is recyclable because the EVOH is <5% of total structure. We also offer mono-PP and metallized mono-PE options that are 100% recyclable while providing excellent barrier properties.
faq.q2: What's the difference between EVOH and aluminum barrier?
faq.a2: EVOH is a plastic-based oxygen barrier that allows the packaging to remain recyclable. Aluminum provides similar barrier but makes packaging non-recyclable. Our EVOH structures offer comparable performance with recyclability.
faq.q3: Do I need high barrier for coffee?
faq.a3: For ground coffee and specialty whole beans, yes - high barrier with degassing valve is recommended. For standard whole bean coffee with quick turnover, medium barrier may suffice. We offer free testing to determine optimal choice.
faq.q4: What certifications do your high barrier materials have?
faq.a4: Our high barrier materials are food-safe certified (FDA, EU 10/2011), and we provide OTR/MVTR test certificates. PCR options are GRS certified. We can provide custom testing documentation for your quality requirements.

cta.title: Protect Your Premium Products
cta.description: Request free OTR/MVTR testing and samples to validate high barrier performance for your products.

lowBarrier.title: Low Barrier Packaging | Eco-Friendly 3-6 Month Shelf Life Pouches
lowBarrier.description: Low barrier eco-friendly packaging for products with 3-6 month shelf life. Home compostable Kraft+PLA, NatureFlex cellulose. Perfect for cookies, tea, granola, and dry goods.
lowBarrier.heroSubtitle: Best eco-credentials with 3-6 month shelf life. Home compostable and recyclable options for sustainable brands.
lowBarrier.introSummary: Low barrier packaging offers the best sustainability credentials with 3-6 months shelf life protection. Perfect for products with fast turnover or naturally dry goods that don't require extended protection.

faq.q1: Is low barrier packaging suitable for my product?
faq.a1: Low barrier is ideal for dry products consumed within 3-6 months: cookies, tea, granola, dried fruits, rice, and similar dry goods. If your product is moisture or oxygen sensitive, consider medium or high barrier options.
faq.q2: Can low barrier packaging be compostable?
faq.a2: Yes! Many low barrier options like Kraft+PLA and NatureFlex are home compostable, breaking down in backyard compost within 6-12 months. These are certified to EN 13432 and OK Home standards.
faq.q3: What's the price difference vs medium barrier?
faq.a3: Low barrier materials typically cost 15-25% less than medium barrier options. The cost savings plus better eco-credentials make it an excellent choice when your product doesn't require extended shelf life.
faq.q4: How do I transition from plastic to low barrier eco-packaging?
faq.a4: We offer free consultation and samples to test compatibility. Many brands successfully switch dry goods to low barrier compostable packaging. We help you validate shelf life and optimize the transition.

cta.title: Start Your Eco Journey
cta.description: Request samples of our home compostable low barrier packaging.

mediumBarrier.title: Medium Barrier Packaging | 6-12 Month Shelf Life Recyclable Pouches
mediumBarrier.description: Medium barrier eco-friendly packaging for products with 6-12 month shelf life. Recyclable mono-PE, Bio-PE, PCR options. Perfect for coffee, nuts, pet food, and protein powders.
mediumBarrier.heroSubtitle: Balanced protection with 6-12 month shelf life. Recyclable options for most food products.
mediumBarrier.introSummary: Medium barrier packaging provides balanced protection with 6-12 months shelf life. The sweet spot for most food products, combining good barrier properties with excellent sustainability credentials.

faq.q1: What products work best with medium barrier?
faq.a1: Medium barrier is ideal for coffee beans, nuts, pet food, protein powders, cereals, and chocolates - products needing 6-12 month protection. It's the most popular choice for food products requiring both freshness and sustainability.
faq.q2: Is mono-PE recyclable everywhere?
faq.a2: Mono-PE (polyethylene) is one of the most widely recycled plastics globally. It's accepted in most curbside recycling programs and store drop-off recycling. We provide recycling instructions for consumers on packaging.
faq.q3: How does Bio-PE compare to regular PE?
faq.a3: Bio-PE (from sugarcane) has identical barrier and sealing properties to fossil-based PE. The difference is environmental: Bio-PE is carbon-negative in production, meaning it removes CO2 during sugarcane growth.
faq.q4: Can I use medium barrier for coffee?
faq.a4: Yes! Medium barrier works well for roasted whole beans with standard turnover. For ground coffee or specialty roasts requiring maximum freshness, consider our high barrier options with degassing valves.

cta.title: Find Your Balance
cta.description: Request samples to test medium barrier protection for your products.

allOptions.title: All Packaging Options | Complete Customization Guide
allOptions.description: Explore all sustainable packaging options: materials, pouch styles, barrier levels, closures, printing, and finishes. Complete customization guide from Achieve Pack.
allOptions.heroSubtitle: Your complete guide to sustainable packaging customization. Explore every option available—from materials to finishes, closures to printing.
allOptions.introSummary: Achieve Pack offers extensive customization options for eco-friendly flexible packaging. This comprehensive guide covers all available materials, pouch styles, barrier levels, closures, printing methods, and special features.

faq.q1: How do I choose between so many options?
faq.a1: Start with your product requirements: What are you packaging? What shelf life do you need? What sustainability goals do you have? Our team can then recommend the best combination of material, style, and features.
faq.q2: Can I mix and match options?
faq.a2: Yes! Most options can be combined. For example, you can have a compostable stand-up pouch with a zipper, matte finish, and spot UV logo. Some combinations have limitations—we'll advise during consultation.
faq.q3: Do all options have the same MOQ?
faq.a3: MOQ varies by option. Digital printing starts at 100 pieces, while plate printing typically requires 5,000+. Complex features may have higher minimums. We offer flexibility for testing.
faq.q4: How do I get samples of different options?
faq.a4: Request a sample kit with examples of different materials, finishes, and features. We can also produce custom samples of your specific design before full production.

cta.title: Build Your Custom Package
cta.description: Work with our specialists to design the perfect packaging for your product.

sizeGuide.title: Pouch Size Guide | Dimensions & Capacity Reference
sizeGuide.description: Complete pouch size guide with dimensions and capacity for stand-up pouches, flat bottom bags, and more. Find the perfect size for your product.
sizeGuide.heroSubtitle: Find the perfect pouch size for your product. Complete dimension guide with capacity references and custom size options.
sizeGuide.introSummary: Selecting the right pouch size ensures optimal product presentation and cost efficiency. Use this comprehensive guide to understand standard sizes and find the perfect fit for your product.

faq.q1: Do you have standard sizes or is everything custom?
faq.a1: We have a range of standard sizes that cover most needs with lower MOQs. Custom sizes are available for orders of 500+ pieces (digital) or 5,000+ pieces (plate printing).
faq.q2: How do I know what size fits my product?
faq.a2: Send us your product details (weight, dimensions, density) or a sample. We'll recommend sizes and can send blank pouches for you to test fill before ordering printed versions.
faq.q3: Are dimensions exact or approximate?
faq.a3: Dimensions are nominal with standard manufacturing tolerance of ±1-2mm. Critical applications should account for this variance.
faq.q4: Can I get the same product in multiple sizes?
faq.a4: Absolutely! Many brands offer the same product in different sizes (sample, regular, family size). Each size can share design elements while using size-specific artwork.

cta.title: Need Help Choosing?
cta.description: Send us your product details and we'll recommend the optimal size.

printingTypes.title: Printing Types Explained | Digital vs Plate Printing
printingTypes.description: Compare digital and plate printing for flexible packaging. Understand MOQs, costs, color options, and which method suits your needs.
printingTypes.heroSubtitle: Understand your printing options. Compare digital and plate printing to choose the right method for your packaging project.
printingTypes.introSummary: Achieve Pack offers digital printing for low MOQ orders and plate (flexographic) printing for high-volume production. Each method has distinct advantages—this guide helps you choose the right option.

faq.q1: Which printing method should I choose?
faq.a1: For orders under 5,000 pieces, digital is usually more cost-effective. For 5,000+, compare total costs including plates. If you need exact Pantone matching or special inks, plate printing is required.
faq.q2: Can I switch from digital to plate printing later?
faq.a2: Yes! Many customers start with digital for market testing, then switch to plate printing once they have consistent demand. We'll help you transition smoothly.
faq.q3: How accurate is digital color matching?
faq.a3: Digital printing simulates Pantone colors using CMYK, which is very close but not exact. For most applications it's excellent. Brand-critical colors (like specific logo colors) may benefit from plate printing.
faq.q4: What's included in plate costs?
faq.a4: Plate costs cover the creation of printing plates for each color in your design. Once made, plates can be reused for repeat orders. We store plates for 2 years at no charge.

cta.title: Get a Printing Quote
cta.description: Tell us about your project and we'll recommend the best printing option.

workflow.title: Our Workflow | From Quote to Delivery
workflow.description: Understand Achieve Pack's packaging production workflow. From initial consultation to final delivery—what to expect at every stage.
workflow.heroSubtitle: A clear, transparent process from initial inquiry to packaging delivery. Know what to expect at every stage.
workflow.introSummary: Our 5-step workflow ensures a smooth experience: Consultation → Design → Sample → Production → Delivery. Typical timeline is 8-12 weeks from inquiry to delivered packaging.

faq.q1: Can the timeline be shortened?
faq.a1: Yes, rush orders are possible with express production and air freight. Digital printing is faster than plate printing. Contact us for urgent timelines—we'll do our best to accommodate.
faq.q2: Do I need to pay upfront?
faq.a2: Typically, we require 50% deposit to start production, with the balance due before shipping. Sample orders may require full payment upfront. We accept bank transfer and major credit cards.
faq.q3: What if I need to make changes after approval?
faq.a3: Changes before production starts are usually possible. Once production begins, changes may incur additional costs or delays. We always confirm final approval before starting.
faq.q4: Do you handle customs and import duties?
faq.a4: We provide all export documentation. For DDP (Delivered Duty Paid), we handle customs clearance. For other terms (FOB, CIF), you or your freight forwarder handle import procedures.

cta.title: Start Your Project
cta.description: Ready to begin? Contact us for a free consultation and quote.

kraftHighBarrier.title: Kraft High Barrier Packaging | Premium Long Shelf Life Pouches
kraftHighBarrier.description: Kraft high barrier packaging with aluminum foil for maximum protection. 12-24+ month shelf life for premium coffee, pharmaceuticals, and freeze-dried foods. FDA certified.
kraftHighBarrier.heroSubtitle: Maximum protection with premium kraft aesthetics. Aluminum foil barrier for 12-24+ month shelf life. Perfect for specialty coffee, pharmaceuticals, and premium foods.
kraftHighBarrier.introSummary: Kraft high barrier packaging features thick aluminum foil layers that provide pharmaceutical-grade protection for oxygen-sensitive products, delivering 12-24+ months shelf life while maintaining the premium natural aesthetic of kraft paper.

faq.q1: How long can products stay fresh in kraft high barrier packaging?
faq.a1: Products can maintain freshness for 12-24+ months depending on the product type and storage conditions. The aluminum foil barrier provides maximum protection against oxygen, moisture, and light degradation.
faq.q2: Is kraft high barrier packaging worth the extra cost?
faq.a2: For premium products, long shelf life requirements, or export markets, absolutely. The extended shelf life reduces waste, allows broader distribution, and enhances perceived value.
faq.q3: Can this packaging be used for pharmaceuticals?
faq.a3: Yes, our kraft high barrier packaging meets pharmaceutical-grade requirements. We can provide all necessary certifications and documentation for regulated products.
faq.q4: What printing options work best on kraft high barrier?
faq.a4: Plate printing provides the most cost-effective solution for high barrier packaging due to typical higher MOQs (5,000-10,000 pcs). Digital printing is available for smaller runs with premium pricing.

cta.title: Discuss Your High Barrier Packaging Needs
cta.description: Let's create premium kraft packaging with maximum protection for your high-value products.

kraftMediumBarrier.title: Kraft Medium Barrier Packaging | Coffee & Premium Food Pouches
kraftMediumBarrier.description: Kraft medium barrier packaging with metallized layers for coffee, nuts, and premium foods. 6-12 month shelf life. Natural aesthetic with enhanced protection. MOQ 1,000 pcs.
kraftMediumBarrier.heroSubtitle: Enhanced kraft paper packaging with metallized barrier layers for coffee, premium snacks, and moderately sensitive products. Natural look meets functional protection.
kraftMediumBarrier.introSummary: Kraft medium barrier packaging combines the rustic appeal of kraft paper with metallized or aluminum barrier layers, providing 6-12 months shelf life for coffee, nuts, dried fruits, and specialty foods.

faq.q1: What is the difference between medium and high barrier kraft packaging?
faq.a1: Medium barrier uses thinner metallized or aluminum layers (12-15µm) providing 6-12 months shelf life, while high barrier uses thicker aluminum (25-40µm) for 12-24+ months.
faq.q2: Can I use kraft medium barrier for coffee packaging?
faq.a2: Yes! It's ideal for coffee with 6-9 month shelf life targets. We can add degassing valves to release CO2 from freshly roasted beans while preventing oxygen entry.
faq.q3: Is the metallized layer recyclable?
faq.a3: The kraft paper component can be recycled, but metallized layers may require specialized recycling facilities. We recommend checking local recycling capabilities.
faq.q4: What printing options are available?
faq.a4: Both digital printing (500+ MOQ) and plate printing (5,000+ MOQ) are available. Kraft paper provides excellent ink adhesion and vibrant colors.

cta.title: Start Your Coffee Packaging Project
cta.description: Let's create premium kraft packaging that protects your products and showcases your brand values.

kraftLowBarrier.title: Kraft Low Barrier Packaging | Eco-Friendly Paper Pouches
kraftLowBarrier.description: Kraft low barrier packaging for dry goods, bakery items, and short shelf life products. Natural paper appearance with basic moisture protection. MOQ 500 pcs.
kraftLowBarrier.heroSubtitle: Natural kraft paper pouches with basic protection for dry goods and short shelf life products. Eco-friendly, cost-effective, and beautifully rustic.
kraftLowBarrier.introSummary: Kraft low barrier packaging combines the natural aesthetic of kraft paper with basic moisture and oxygen protection, perfect for bakery items, dry snacks, and products with 3-6 month shelf life requirements.

faq.q1: What is the shelf life for products in kraft low barrier packaging?
faq.a1: Typically 3-6 months for dry, low-moisture products. The exact shelf life depends on the product type, storage conditions, and moisture content.
faq.q2: Can kraft low barrier packaging be used for coffee?
faq.a2: Yes, but only for short-term storage (2-3 months). For longer shelf life or premium coffee, consider our kraft medium or high barrier options with degassing valves.
faq.q3: Is this packaging food safe?
faq.a3: Yes, all our kraft packaging materials are FDA compliant and food-safe. The inner PE coating prevents direct contact between food and paper fibers.
faq.q4: What is the minimum order quantity?
faq.a4: For digital printing: 500-1,000 pieces. For plate printing: 5,000-10,000 pieces depending on size and design complexity.

cta.title: Get Started with Kraft Low Barrier Packaging
cta.description: Let's create sustainable kraft paper packaging that showcases your brand's natural values.

csCoffeeRoastery.title: Coffee Roastery Case Study | Bean & Bole Compostable Packaging
csCoffeeRoastery.description: How Bean & Bole Coffee Roastery switched to compostable packaging while maintaining freshness. Case study featuring Kraft+PLA pouches with degassing valves.
csCoffeeRoastery.heroTitle: Case Study: Bean & Bole Coffee Roastery
csCoffeeRoastery.heroSubtitle: How a specialty coffee roaster achieved 100% compostable packaging without compromising freshness or their small-batch approach.
csCoffeeRoastery.introSummary: Bean & Bole Coffee Roastery partnered with Achieve Pack to transition from conventional plastic to certified compostable packaging. The result: eco-friendly bags that maintain coffee freshness while reinforcing their sustainability story.

faq.q1: Can compostable packaging maintain coffee freshness?
faq.a1: Yes! With the right material combination (like Kraft + PLA with proper barrier layers), compostable packaging can maintain coffee freshness for 3-4 months. Adding a degassing valve allows CO2 release while preventing oxygen entry.
faq.q2: What was the minimum order for this project?
faq.a2: Bean & Bole started with 500 bags per SKU using digital printing. This allowed them to test the packaging with customers before committing to larger quantities.
faq.q3: Is the degassing valve also compostable?
faq.a3: We offer both options. Standard degassing valves are not compostable and need to be removed before composting—we clearly label this on the package. Fully compostable valve options are available but cost 2-3x more than standard coffee valves.

cta.title: Ready for Your Sustainable Packaging Story?
cta.description: Let's discuss how we can help your coffee brand transition to eco-friendly packaging.

csTeaBrand.introSummary: Milano Botanica partnered with Achieve Pack to create premium compostable packaging with transparent windows. The result: beautiful packaging that showcases their teas while meeting EU sustainability requirements.

faq.q1: Is the window also compostable?
faq.a1: Yes! We used NatureFlex™, a cellulose-based transparent film that is certified compostable. The entire package including the window can go into commercial composting.
faq.q2: How does this meet EU PPWR requirements?
faq.a2: The packaging is certified compostable under EN 13432, which is one pathway to EU PPWR compliance. Full documentation is provided for retailer and regulatory requirements.
faq.q3: What shelf life can tea achieve in this packaging?
faq.a3: Depending on the tea type, this packaging structure provides 9-12 months shelf life. The NatureFlex window maintains good moisture barrier while allowing product visibility.

csSuperfood.introSummary: VitalGreen Superfoods partnered with Achieve Pack to develop recyclable high-barrier packaging for their superfood powders. The GRS-certified solution helped them gain B-Corp points while protecting product quality.

faq.q1: How can recyclable packaging achieve high barrier?
faq.a1: Our mono-PE structure incorporates a thin EVOH layer that provides excellent oxygen barrier while maintaining recyclability in PE recycling streams. The package is store drop-off recyclable.
faq.q2: What documentation do you provide for B-Corp reporting?
faq.a2: We provide GRS certificates, material specifications, carbon footprint estimates, and recyclability statements that can be used for B-Corp sustainability reporting.
faq.q3: How did they launch 8 SKUs at once?

csPetTreats.introSummary: Pawsome Naturals partnered with Achieve Pack to create PCR-based packaging with child-resistant zippers, meeting retailer requirements while reinforcing their sustainability message.

faq.q1: Is the child-resistant closure pet-proof?

faq.q2: What makes PCR packaging sustainable?

csChocolate.title: Chocolate Brand Case Study | Artisan Cocoa Luxury Packaging

csChocolate.heroSubtitle: How a luxury chocolate brand achieved premium packaging that withstands Middle East heat.
csChocolate.introSummary: Artisan Cocoa Emirates partnered with Achieve Pack to create luxury packaging that protects chocolate in extreme heat while conveying premium brand values.

faq.q1: Can flexible packaging protect chocolate in hot climates?

faq.q2: Is foil stamping sustainable?

csCandle.title: Candle Brand Case Study | Luminara Sustainable Packaging

csCandle.heroSubtitle: How an artisan candle brand achieved compostable packaging that preserves fragrance and appeals to boutique retailers.
csCandle.introSummary: Luminara Candles partnered with Achieve Pack to create compostable kraft packaging with aroma barriers, enabling small-batch seasonal releases.

csBakery.heroSubtitle: How an artisan bakery achieved home compostable packaging that sells at farmers markets.
csBakery.introSummary: Wholesome Bakes partnered with Achieve Pack to create fully home compostable packaging with windows, perfect for farmers market sales and eco-conscious consumers.

csWellness.description: How NourishNow Wellness achieved e-commerce ready bio-PE supplement packaging with child-resistant closures.

csWellness.introSummary: NourishNow Wellness partnered with Achieve Pack to create bio-PE packaging optimized for e-commerce, with child-resistant closures and UV protection.

faq.q1: What is Bio-PE?
faq.a1: Bio-PE is polyethylene made from sugarcane ethanol instead of petroleum. It has identical performance to conventional PE but is made from renewable resources.
faq.q2: Does it meet Amazon FBA requirements?

csOrganicNuts.title: Organic Nuts Case Study | NutriVie EU PPWR Compliant Packaging
csOrganicNuts.description: How NutriVie Organic achieved EU PPWR compliant nut packaging with 12-month shelf life and unified design system.
csOrganicNuts.heroSubtitle: How an organic nut brand achieved EU PPWR compliance while reducing packaging costs across 15 SKUs.
csOrganicNuts.introSummary: NutriVie Organic partnered with Achieve Pack to create EU PPWR-compliant recyclable packaging with a unified design system that reduced costs across their 15-SKU range.

faq.q1: How does one plate set work for 15 SKUs?

csBathProducts.title: Bath Products Case Study | Bavarian Bliss Clear Packaging
csBathProducts.description: How Bavarian Bliss achieved moisture-protected clear packaging for bath bombs with gift-worthy presentation.
csBathProducts.heroTitle: Case Study: Bavarian Bliss Bath Co.
csBathProducts.heroSubtitle: How a bath bomb brand achieved clear, moisture-protected packaging that tripled Christmas market sales.
csBathProducts.introSummary: Bavarian Bliss Bath Co. partnered with Achieve Pack to create clear recyclable packaging with high moisture barrier, perfect for showcasing colorful bath bombs at Christmas markets.

faq.q1: How do you prevent bath bomb activation?
faq.a1: The high moisture barrier seal structure prevents humidity from reaching the bath bombs. Properly sealed, products stay fresh for 12+ months even in humid conditions.
faq.q2: Are clear pouches eco-friendly?
faq.a2: Yes! Our clear mono-PE pouches are recyclable in PE recycling streams. The clear material also reduces need for printed windows, using less ink overall.

csAdaptogens.title: Adaptogens Case Study | Zen Botanics Premium Packaging
csAdaptogens.description: How Zen Botanics Asia achieved premium adaptogen packaging with maximum barrier protection for tropical climates.
csAdaptogens.heroSubtitle: How an adaptogen brand achieved premium positioning with matte black packaging that protects against tropical humidity.
csAdaptogens.introSummary: Zen Botanics Asia partnered with Achieve Pack to create premium matte black packaging with maximum barrier protection, perfect for adaptogens in tropical climates.

faq.q1: How long do adaptogens stay potent?
faq.a1: With our high-barrier structure, functional mushrooms and adaptogens maintain potency for 18+ months. The light-blocking matte finish also prevents UV degradation.
faq.q2: Can you do multi-size from one design?

csOutdoorSnacks.heroSubtitle: How an outdoor snack brand achieved REI national listing with durable, eco-certified packaging.
csOutdoorSnacks.introSummary: TrailFuel Adventures partnered with Achieve Pack to create reinforced PCR packaging that survives trail conditions while meeting REI's sustainability requirements.

faq.q1: How does the packaging handle altitude changes?
faq.a1: We test packaging at high altitude (14,000+ ft) to ensure seals do not pop or fail. The flexible structure accommodates pressure changes during mountain retail and trail use.
faq.q2: What makes it puncture-resistant?
faq.a2: The reinforced mono-PE structure is thicker than standard pouches, designed specifically for outdoor use where gear, rocks, and trail conditions can damage packaging.

commercialComposting.manyBrandsKnowThatComposting: Muchas marcas saben que el compostaje es bueno para el clima y el suelo. Pero cuando se les pregunta cómo funciona realmente (cómo es la instalación, cuánto tiempo lleva o si realmente puede procesar envases), la mayoría se queda en blanco.

commercialComposting.manyCertifiedCompostableMaterialsWork: Muchos materiales compostables certificados funcionan sólo en el compostaje industrial, no en pilas de jardín.
commercialComposting.ifYourMarketDoesntHave: . Si su mercado no tiene acceso a una instalación comercial, o si esa instalación no acepta envases, su opción "compostable" sigue siendo teórica en lugar de práctica.
commercialComposting.thisArticleWalksYouThrough: Este artículo lo guía a través de tres métodos principales de compostaje comercial, explica cómo funciona cada uno y muestra exactamente dónde
commercialComposting.fitsInByTheEnd: encaja. Al final, sabrá cómo funciona el sistema y cómo Achievepack diseña embalajes para instalaciones reales.
commercialComposting.commercialCompostingUsesTheExact: El compostaje comercial utiliza exactamente la misma biología que una pila de abono de jardín (microorganismos que descomponen el material orgánico en abono estable y rico en nutrientes), pero ampliado y cuidadosamente controlado. Las instalaciones gestionan enormes volúmenes de restos de comida, recortes de jardín y otros productos orgánicos utilizando equipos para regularlos.
commercialComposting.soDecompositionHappensQuicklyAnd: para que la descomposición se produzca de forma rápida y segura.
commercialComposting.comparedToLandfillingOrIncineration: En comparación con los vertederos o la incineración, el compostaje industrial frena el crecimiento de los vertederos, reduce las emisiones de gases de efecto invernadero y reduce la necesidad de fertilizantes sintéticos al convertir los desechos en recursos para la formación del suelo. Es el ambiente el que
commercialComposting.organicMaterialComesFromMultiple: El material orgánico proviene de múltiples fuentes: programas residenciales de recolección en las aceras, cocinas comerciales, restaurantes, tiendas de comestibles, plantas de procesamiento de alimentos y, cada vez más, envases compostables certificados. Una sola instalación grande puede procesar
commercialComposting.beforeMaterialEverEntersA: Antes de que el material ingrese a un sistema de compostaje, debe recolectarse y clasificarse.
commercialComposting.mostCommercialFacilitiesPartnerWith: La mayoría de las instalaciones comerciales se asocian con municipios o transportistas de residuos que recolectan material orgánico de programas en las aceras, de la misma manera que funciona la recolección de basura, excepto que se separan en un contenedor verde o marrón. Algunas instalaciones también aceptan material directamente: restaurantes y empresas de alimentación traen sus sobras; Los consumidores individuales pueden dejar los desechos de jardín y los compostables en
commercialComposting.onlyCertifiedCompostablePackagingmeeting: Solo envases compostables certificados que cumplan con estándares como
commercialComposting.shouldEnterTheSystemAlongside: —deberían ingresar al sistema junto con los restos de comida. Si el embalaje tiene la etiqueta "compostable" pero no está certificado, las instalaciones lo rechazarán o contaminarán el abono terminado con microplásticos.
commercialComposting.windrowSystemsAreSimpleCosteffective: Los sistemas de hileras son simples, rentables y capaces de manejar grandes volúmenes.
commercialComposting.operatorsRegularlyTurnThePiles: Los operadores voltean periódicamente los pilotes con maquinaria.
commercialComposting.toEnsureAllMaterialGets: para garantizar que todo el material reciba calor, humedad y oxígeno.
commercialComposting.thatsWhyYoullFindWindrow: Es por eso que encontrará sistemas de hileras en instalaciones municipales y operaciones agrícolas en toda América del Norte.
commercialComposting.method2AeratedStaticPile1: Método 2: compostaje en pilas estáticas aireadas
commercialComposting.aeratedStaticPileCompostingMixes: El compostaje de pilas estáticas aireadas mezcla materia orgánica con materiales más gruesos como astillas de madera o papel triturado para crear bolsas de aire y mejorar el flujo de oxígeno. Algunas instalaciones añaden
commercialComposting.toActivelyPushAirInto: para empujar activamente el aire hacia la masa.
commercialComposting.theTradeoffIsSelectivityAerated: La desventaja es la selectividad: los sistemas aireados no pueden manejar ciertos materiales (como altos niveles de grasas o aceites) que las hileras pueden manejar.
commercialComposting.presortingBecomesMoreCritical: La clasificación previa se vuelve más crítica.
commercialComposting.invesselCompostingRepresentsTheHighest: El compostaje en recipiente representa el nivel más alto de control del proceso. Los orgánicos triturados y mezclados se colocan en
commercialComposting.enclosedContainersOrIndustrialComposting: contenedores cerrados o máquinas de compostaje industrial
commercialComposting.whereTemperatureHydrationAndAeration: donde la temperatura, la hidratación y la aireación están estrictamente controladas por sistemas automatizados.
commercialComposting.theDownsideIsHigherUpfront: La desventaja es una mayor inversión de capital inicial. Pero para operaciones comerciales a gran escala, la velocidad y confiabilidad hacen que los sistemas a bordo de embarcaciones valga la pena.

commercialComposting.isOnlyMeaningfulIfPeople: " sólo tiene sentido si la gente sabe dónde y cómo usarlo. Etiquete su embalaje con instrucciones:
commercialComposting.compostAtYourLocalCommercial: "Abono en sus instalaciones comerciales locales. Compostable en 90 a 180 días".
commercialComposting.toHelpYouAssessYour: para ayudarle a evaluar su infraestructura local y decidir si los materiales compostables certificados son los más adecuados.
commercialComposting.commercialCompostingUsesTheSame: El compostaje comercial utiliza la misma biología que el compostaje de jardín (microorganismos que descomponen el material orgánico), pero ampliado y cuidadosamente controlado. Las instalaciones gestionan enormes volúmenes de restos de comida, recortes de jardín y otros productos orgánicos utilizando equipos para regular la temperatura, el oxígeno y la humedad para que la descomposición se produzca de forma rápida y segura.
commercialComposting.whatAreTheThreeMain: ¿Cuáles son los tres principales métodos comerciales de compostaje?
commercialComposting.theThreePrimaryMethodsAre: Los tres métodos principales son: 1) Compostaje en hileras: material dispuesto en hileras largas y volteado periódicamente, lo que lleva aproximadamente 4 meses; 2) Pila Estática Aireada - material mezclado con astillas de madera con aire forzado, finalizándose en 3 meses; 3) Compostaje en recipiente: contenedores cerrados con control automatizado, que finalizan en menos de un mes.
commercialComposting.canCompostablePackagingBeProcessed: ¿Se pueden procesar envases compostables en instalaciones comerciales?
commercialComposting.yesButOnlyCertifiedCompostable: Sí, pero solo deben ingresar al sistema envases compostables certificados que cumplan con estándares como EN 13432 o ASTM D6400. Estas certificaciones garantizan que los materiales se biodegradarán completamente en un plazo de 180 días a temperaturas de instalaciones industriales (140 °F/60 °C o más).
commercialComposting.processingTimeVariesByMethod: El tiempo de procesamiento varía según el método: el compostaje en hileras tarda aproximadamente 4 meses, los sistemas de pilas estáticas aireadas se completan en aproximadamente 3 meses y el compostaje en recipientes puede producir compost terminado en menos de un mes.
commercialComposting.achievepackDesignsCertifiedCompostablePa: Achievepack® diseña envases compostables certificados para tostadores de café, máquinas para preparar refrigerios, golosinas para mascotas, suplementos y otras marcas de alimentos y de consumo. Todas nuestras estructuras están certificadas para
commercialComposting.andDesignedForCommercialComposting: y diseñado para infraestructura de compostaje comercial. Fabricación certificada BRC desde 2015.

naturalCelluloseFiber.whatIsTheBestSustainable: ¿Cuál es el mejor material de embalaje sostenible para B2B?
naturalCelluloseFiber.naturalCelluloseFiberIsOne:
La fibra de celulosa natural es uno de los mejores materiales de embalaje sostenibles para aplicaciones B2B. 
Derivado de fuentes vegetales renovables como la pulpa de madera, ofrece una biodegradabilidad total (30-90 % en 28-120 días). 
alta resistencia a la tracción (hasta 900 MPa) y menor impacto ambiental que las alternativas plásticas. 
AchievePack ofrece soluciones de celulosa escalables para envases de alimentos, comercio electrónico y productos electrónicos. 
Póngase en contacto con ryan@achievepack.com o visite lograrpack.com.
naturalCelluloseFiber.cellulosePackagingSupplierForBrands: ¿Proveedor de envases de celulosa para marcas?
naturalCelluloseFiber.achievePackIsALeading:
Achieve Pack es un proveedor líder de envases sostenibles que ofrece soluciones de fibra de celulosa natural. 
para marcas B2B. Nuestros envases de celulosa se derivan de fuentes vegetales renovables, totalmente biodegradables, 
y cumple con las regulaciones globales de prohibición del plástico. Ofrecemos soluciones OEM escalables con MOQ bajo para alimentos, 
comercio electrónico y embalaje de productos electrónicos. Póngase en contacto con ryan@achievepack.com o visite lograrpack.com.
naturalCelluloseFiber.question_whatIsNaturalCelluloseFiber: ¿Qué es la fibra de celulosa natural en los envases?
naturalCelluloseFiber.answer_naturalCelluloseFibersAreDerived: Las fibras de celulosa natural se derivan directamente de fuentes vegetales renovables como la pulpa de madera. A diferencia de las fibras de acetato modificadas químicamente, la celulosa natural conserva su estructura molecular original, lo que ofrece total biodegradabilidad y compostabilidad. Estas fibras presentan unidades de D-glucosa unidas β(1→4) con alta resistencia a la tracción (hasta 900 MPa).
naturalCelluloseFiber.question_howDoesCelluloseCompareTo: ¿Cómo se compara la celulosa con la fibra de acetato?
naturalCelluloseFiber.answer_celluloseIgnitesFastBurnsWith: La celulosa se enciende rápidamente, arde con olor a papel y deja una suave ceniza gris que se desmorona fácilmente. El acetato emite un olor a vinagre, forma perlas negras duras y se derrite parcialmente. La celulosa se biodegrada entre un 30% y un 90% en 28-120 días en compostaje, mientras que el acetato se degrada menos del 3% en 16 semanas.
naturalCelluloseFiber.question_whatAreTheEnvironmentalBenefits: ¿Cuáles son los beneficios ambientales de los envases de celulosa?
naturalCelluloseFiber.answer_lifeCycleAssessmentsConfirmCellul: Las evaluaciones del ciclo de vida confirman que los envases de celulosa tienen un menor potencial de calentamiento global y ecotoxicidad que el acetato o el PET. Se biodegrada entre un 30% y un 90% en 28-120 días en compostaje y elimina los riesgos de microplásticos asociados con la degradación del acetato. Los envases de celulosa pueden reducir la huella de carbono del comercio electrónico en más de un 30 % en comparación con los plásticos.
naturalCelluloseFiber.question_isCelluloseFiberPackagingFoodsa: ¿Los envases de fibra de celulosa son seguros para los alimentos?
naturalCelluloseFiber.answer_yesNaturalCelluloseFiberPackaging: Sí, los envases de fibra de celulosa natural son aptos para alimentos y adecuados para aplicaciones de protección. Proporciona una fuerte retención de agua, resistencia a las grietas y barreras contra la humedad. Las soluciones de celulosa de AchievePack cumplen con los estándares globales de seguridad alimentaria y con las prohibiciones de plástico de un solo uso en varios mercados.
naturalCelluloseFiber.question_whatApplicationsIsCellulosePack: ¿Para qué aplicaciones son mejores los envases de celulosa?
naturalCelluloseFiber.answer_cellulosePackagingIsIdealFor: Los envases de celulosa son ideales para envases de alimentos, comercio electrónico y productos electrónicos. Su alta resistencia a la tracción a nivel de microfibrillas (hasta 900 MPa) y su módulo elástico (hasta 8,5 GPa) lo hacen perfecto para embalajes de pulpa moldeada y aplicaciones de protección que requieren sostenibilidad y rendimiento.

plasticFree.alt_plasticfreeVsCompostablePackagingCom: Comparación de envases sin plástico y compostables
plasticFree.metaDescription: Conozca la diferencia entre envases sin plástico y compostables. Guía de expertos sobre bioplásticos PLA y PBAT y cómo posicionar con precisión sus afirmaciones sobre envases sostenibles.
plasticFree.metaKeywords: envases sin plástico, compostables vs sin plástico, envases PLA, bioplástico PBAT, sin plástico convencional, envases de base biológica, EN 13432, declaraciones de envases sostenibles
plasticFree.metaTitle: Envases sin plástico versus envases compostables: comprender la diferencia | Paquete de logros
plasticFree.plasticfreeVsCompostableUnderstandingThe: Sin plástico versus compostable: comprender la diferencia
plasticFree.aClearExplanationOfHow: Una explicación clara de cómo estos conceptos relacionados pero diferentes se aplican a sus opciones de embalaje sostenible.
plasticFree.plasticfreeAndCompostableAreRelated:
"Sin plástico" y "compostable" son conceptos relacionados pero diferentes. Esta guía aclara la diferencia. 
y explica cómo encajan nuestras bolsas compostables multicapa.
plasticFree.meansAMaterialIsDesigned:
significa que un material está diseñado para descomponerse en agua, dióxido de carbono, 
y biomasa en condiciones definidas de compostaje, sin dejar residuos tóxicos.
plasticFree.notAutomaticallyMeanTheProduct: no significa automáticamente que el producto esté libre de plástico
plasticFree.becauseManyCompostableMaterialsLike:
, porque muchos compostables 
Los materiales (como PLA o PBAT) siguen siendo plásticos: son solo polímeros biodegradables en lugar de 
PE o PP convencionales.
plasticFree.inTheStrictSenseMeans:
en sentido estricto significa nada de polímeros plásticos. Esto normalmente 
limita los materiales a papel, cartón y fibras naturales como el bambú o el bagazo, sin 
añadidos bioplásticos como PLA o PBAT en cualquier capa o recubrimiento.
plasticFree.thisIsWhereCertificationLanguage: Aquí es donde el lenguaje de certificación cobra importancia:
plasticFree.noPlasticComponentsWhetherFossilbased:
Sin componentes plásticos, ya sean de origen fósil o biológico. Un producto que contiene PLA o PBAT 
haría
plasticFree.hereEvenIfFullyCompostable: aquí, incluso si es totalmente compostable.
plasticFree.allowsBiobasedOrCompostablePolymers:
Permite polímeros de base biológica o compostables y excluye los plásticos fósiles tradicionales como PE, PP, PET. 
Bajo este alcance, PLA y PBAT se aceptan como "bioplásticos", por lo que el producto puede estar libre de 
plásticos convencionales aunque todavía contenga polímeros plásticos.
plasticFree.ourPlaPbatLayerFits:
Nuestra capa PLA + PBAT se ajusta al concepto "convencional sin plástico": 
es una mezcla de bioplásticos compostables que reemplaza los plásticos fósiles tradicionales pero que no "no contiene plástico en absoluto".
plasticFree.ourCompostableBagHas3: Nuestra bolsa compostable tiene 3 capas funcionales más tintas y adhesivo compostables:
plasticFree.compostablePlasticfreeAndBiobasedMade: Compostable, sin plástico y de base biológica; elaborado a partir de fibra de papel y totalmente compatible con los sistemas de compostaje.
plasticFree.aluminumCoatingOnPlaAluminum:
Recubrimiento de aluminio sobre PLA. El aluminio en sí es un metal, no un plástico, y se considera inerte y 
no es compostable, pero no es perjudicial en capas traza; El soporte PLA es un bioplástico compostable.
plasticFree.overallPerformanceStillTargetsIndustrial:
El rendimiento general todavía apunta a la compostabilidad industrial porque la capa de PLA está diseñada para 
biodegradarse y la capa de metal es extremadamente delgada.
plasticFree.compostableBiobasedPolymerMadeFrom: Polímero compostable de base biológica elaborado a partir de recursos renovables como el maíz o la caña de azúcar; Es un plástico, pero no un plástico fósil convencional.
plasticFree.compostablePolyesterMadeFromPetrochemica: Poliéster compostable elaborado a partir de bloques de construcción petroquímicos; A base de petróleo pero diseñado para biodegradarse en condiciones de compostaje, a menudo mezclado con PLA para mayor flexibilidad y dureza.
plasticFree.selectedAsCompostableSystemsCompatible:
Seleccionados como sistemas compostables compatibles con los estándares de compost industrial; todavía pueden contener 
componentes derivados del petróleo, pero están formulados para cumplir con los límites de compostabilidad y desintegrarse de manera segura.
plasticFree.inTheStrictZeroPolymer: en el sentido estricto de "polímero cero", gracias al PLA y al PBAT
plasticFree.petroleumbasedVsBiobasedContent: Contenido a base de petróleo versus contenido de base biológica
plasticFree.biobasedAndPetroleumbased: De base biológica y a base de petróleo.
plasticFree.describeWhereTheCarbonComes:
describir de dónde viene el carbono, no si 
algo es compostable.
plasticFree.likePlaAreMadeFrom:
(como el PLA) están hechos de energía renovable 
biomasa y pueden ser compostables, pero no dejan de ser plásticos.
plasticFree.inOurStructureCanContain:
en nuestra estructura puede contener 
carbono a base de petróleo aún así estar certificado como compostable cuando cumple con estándares como 
EN 13432 o ASTM D6400.
plasticFree.biobasedCertificationLevels: Niveles de certificación de base biológica

plasticFree.question_whatIsTheDifferenceBetween: ¿Cuál es la diferencia entre envases sin plástico y compostables?
plasticFree.answer_plasticfreeStrictSenseMeansAbsolu: Sin plástico (en sentido estricto) significa absolutamente nada de polímeros plásticos, incluidos los bioplásticos como PLA o PBAT. Compostable significa que el material se descompone en un plazo de 90 a 180 días en condiciones de compostaje, pero aún puede contener bioplásticos compostables. Nuestras bolsas son compostables y no contienen plástico convencional, pero no están estrictamente libres de plástico debido a las capas de PLA/PBAT.
plasticFree.answer_yesPlaPolylacticAcidIs: Sí, el PLA (ácido poliláctico) es técnicamente un plástico: es un polímero biodegradable. Sin embargo, es un plástico biodegradable y compostable elaborado a partir de recursos renovables como el maíz o la caña de azúcar, no un plástico convencional de origen fósil como el PE o el PP. Por eso nuestras bolsas están "libres de plástico convencional", pero no "libres de plástico" en el sentido más estricto.
plasticFree.answer_conventionalPlasticfreeAllowsBiob: Los productos sin plástico convencionales permiten polímeros de base biológica o compostables, excluyendo los plásticos tradicionales de origen fósil como PE, PP, PET. Bajo esta definición, PLA y PBAT se aceptan como alternativas sustentables, lo que hace que nuestras bolsas compostables multicapa califiquen como libres de plástico convencionales.
plasticFree.answer_yesOurCompostableBagsAre: Sí, nuestras bolsas compostables están diseñadas para cumplir con estándares de compostabilidad industrial como EN 13432 y ASTM D6400. Todos los materiales, tintas y adhesivos se seleccionan para que sean compatibles con los sistemas de compostaje industrial. Contáctenos para obtener documentación de certificación específica.
plasticFree.question_whatIsPbatAndWhy: ¿Qué es PBAT y por qué está en tus maletas?
plasticFree.answer_pbatPolybutyleneAdipateTerephthal: PBAT (tereftalato de adipato de polibutileno) es un poliéster compostable que agrega flexibilidad y dureza a la película de PLA. Si bien el PBAT está elaborado a partir de componentes petroquímicos, está diseñado para biodegradarse en condiciones de compostaje. Comúnmente se mezcla con PLA para mejorar las propiedades mecánicas y al mismo tiempo mantener la compostabilidad.
plasticFree.question_isTheAluminumLayerIn: ¿La capa de aluminio de sus bolsas es compostable?
plasticFree.answer_theMetallizedPlaLayerUses: La capa de PLA metalizado utiliza un recubrimiento de aluminio extremadamente delgado para propiedades de barrera. Si bien el aluminio en sí es un metal (no plástico) y no se puede convertir en compost, la capa es tan delgada que el rendimiento general de la bolsa aún apunta a la compostabilidad industrial. El soporte de PLA es totalmente compostable.
plasticFree.question_canYourBagsBeCertified: ¿Se pueden certificar sus bolsas como de base biológica?
plasticFree.answer_ourBagsContainBiobasedComponents: Nuestras bolsas contienen componentes de base biológica (papel kraft, PLA de maíz/caña de azúcar) y componentes derivados del petróleo (PBAT, algunas tintas/adhesivos químicos). Según el programa OK de base biológica de TÜV Austria, los productos pueden variar desde 1 estrella (20-40 % de base biológica) hasta 4 estrellas (≥80 % de base biológica). Nuestras bolsas calificarían para una certificación parcial de base biológica.
plasticFree.question_howShouldIPositionMy: ¿Cómo debo posicionar los reclamos de envases compostables de mi marca?
plasticFree.answer_weRecommendTransparentMessagingCo: Recomendamos mensajes transparentes: 'Envases compostables elaborados con materiales de origen vegetal y bioplásticos compostables. Libre de plásticos convencionales como PE y PP. Diseñado para instalaciones de compostaje industrial.' Esto evita el lavado verde y al mismo tiempo resalta los beneficios genuinos de la sostenibilidad.

carbonNeutralBags.question_whatIsCarbonneutralPackaging: ¿Qué son los envases neutros en carbono?
carbonNeutralBags.answer_carbonneutralPackagingMeansTheTot: Los envases neutros en carbono significan que las emisiones totales de carbono derivadas de la producción del envase se equilibran a cero neto. Esto se logra mediante una combinación del uso de materiales bajos en carbono (como PCR PE, películas de origen biológico), procesos de producción eficientes y la compra de compensaciones de carbono certificadas para compensar las emisiones restantes.
carbonNeutralBags.question_howDoYouCalculateThe: ¿Cómo se calcula la huella de carbono de los envases?
carbonNeutralBags.answer_weUseLifecycleAssessmentLca: Utilizamos la metodología de análisis del ciclo de vida (LCA) para calcular la huella de carbono, considerando la extracción de materia prima, la fabricación, el transporte y el final de vida. Estos datos nos ayudan a identificar oportunidades de reducción y determinar la cantidad de compensaciones de carbono necesarias para lograr la neutralidad.
carbonNeutralBags.question_whatCarbonOffsetProjectsDo: ¿Qué proyectos de compensación de carbono apoya?
carbonNeutralBags.answer_weWorkWithCertifiedCarbon: Trabajamos con proyectos de compensación de carbono certificados que cumplen con estándares internacionales como Gold Standard o Verified Carbon Standard (VCS). Estos suelen incluir proyectos de reforestación, energía renovable y sostenibilidad comunitaria. También somos miembros de la iniciativa 1% para el Planeta de Stripe Climate.
carbonNeutralBags.question_canIDisplayACarbonneutral: ¿Puedo mostrar un logotipo de carbono neutro en mi embalaje?
carbonNeutralBags.answer_yesWhenYouChooseOur: ¡Sí! Cuando elige nuestras soluciones de embalaje neutras en carbono, puede mostrar marcas de certificación neutras en carbono en sus productos, lo que ayuda a comunicar su compromiso de sostenibilidad a los consumidores y respalda sus informes ESG.
carbonNeutralBags.question_isCarbonneutralPackagingMoreExp: ¿Son más caros los envases neutros en carbono?
carbonNeutralBags.answer_thereIsTypicallyAModest: Por lo general, hay una prima modesta en comparación con los envases convencionales, principalmente debido al costo de las compensaciones de carbono y a los materiales sostenibles potencialmente más costosos. Sin embargo, muchas marcas consideran que el valor de marketing y los beneficios del cumplimiento normativo superan el costo adicional.
carbonNeutralBags.question_whatIsTheMinimumOrder: ¿Cuál es la cantidad mínima de pedido para bolsas neutras en carbono?
carbonNeutralBags.answer_ourMinimumOrderQuantityStarts: Nuestra cantidad mínima de pedido comienza en 1000 piezas para estructuras en stock. Para las bolsas neutras en carbono impresas a medida, la cantidad mínima de pedido suele comenzar en 5000 piezas. Contáctenos para cotizaciones exactas según sus requisitos.
carbonNeutralBags.question_howDoesCarbonneutralPackagingHe: ¿Cómo ayudan los envases neutros en carbono a los informes ESG?
carbonNeutralBags.answer_carbonneutralPackagingProvidesQua: Los envases neutros en carbono proporcionan datos cuantificables para los informes de emisiones de Alcance 3. Proporcionamos documentación de cálculos de huella de carbono y certificados de compensación que pueden incluirse en sus informes de sostenibilidad.
carbonNeutralBags.question_whatMaterialsAreUsedIn: ¿Qué materiales se utilizan en las bolsas neutras en carbono?
carbonNeutralBags.answer_weOfferMultipleOptionsTraditional: Ofrecemos múltiples opciones: PE tradicional con compensaciones de carbono, PE PCR (reciclado posconsumo), PE verde de base biológica procedente de caña de azúcar y estructuras monomateriales reciclables. Cada uno se puede combinar para lograr la neutralidad de carbono. Visite nuestras páginas de materiales para obtener especificaciones detalladas.

heatResistantCandlePackaging.forCustomersWhoMeltWax: Para los clientes que derriten cera directamente en la bolsa, nuestras capas de CPLA evitan que los "puntos calientes" localizados derritan la película, lo que garantiza la seguridad en la estufa y el microondas.
heatResistantCandlePackaging.weDontJustSellBags: "No solo vendemos bolsas; vendemos la seguridad de que su cera a 85°C no terminará en su fábrica. Nuestra experiencia en polímeros compostables a altas temperaturas permite a los fabricantes de velas deshacerse del frasco de vidrio sin perder rendimiento".

heatResistantCandlePackaging.findingTheRightPouchFor: Encontrar la bolsa adecuada para cera a 85°C no es sólo una cuestión de proveedor; se trata de diseñar la pila de materiales perfecta. microwaveSteamBags.forEcoconsciousFoodStartupsAnd: Para las nuevas empresas de alimentos y los clientes minoristas con conciencia ecológica, el enorme volumen de envases de plástico rígido que terminan en los vertederos es una gran preocupación. Al hacer la transición a bolsas flexibles y livianas, su marca puede ofrecer un rendimiento de calefacción superior y, al mismo tiempo, reducir significativamente su huella ambiental.
microwaveSteamBags.atPouchecoWeEngineerMicrowavesafe: En Pouch.eco, diseñamos bolsas para cocinar aptas para microondas utilizando materiales alternativos sostenibles que no comprometen el rendimiento del calentamiento:
microwaveSteamBags.madeFromASinglePolymer: Hecho de una sola clase de polímero diseñado para ir directamente a los programas de reciclaje estándar, eliminando los desechos de plástico mixto.
microwaveSteamBags.becauseTheyShipFlatThese: Debido a que se envían planas, estas bolsas requieren hasta un 80% menos de espacio en el camión de envío que los contenedores rígidos vacíos, lo que ahorra combustible y reduce las emisiones de la carga.
microwaveSteamBags.cookingInsidePlasticCanBe: Cocinar dentro de plástico puede resultar preocupante debido a la lixiviación química. Es por eso que mantenemos nuestros materiales con los más altos estándares regulatorios y de seguridad, utilizando películas compuestas especializadas de alta temperatura y grado alimenticio.
microwaveSteamBags.thirdpartyLabTestedApproved: Laboratorio de terceros probado y aprobado
microwaveSteamBags.ourMicrowaveSteamBagsAre: Nuestras bolsas de vapor para microondas se fabrican en instalaciones certificadas por BRC y se prueban rigurosamente para cumplir con el cumplimiento de la FDA en materia de contacto con alimentos. Están 100 % libres de BPA, plastificantes y están diseñados específicamente para permanecer estructuralmente estables hasta 100 °C sin transferir olores ni compuestos químicos a los alimentos.
microwaveSteamBags.seeFreshnessAtAGlance: Vea la frescura de un vistazo
microwaveSteamBags.theHighclaritySemitransparentFrontWindow: La ventana frontal semitransparente de alta claridad permite a los clientes o chefs monitorear el progreso de la cocción, observar cómo los alimentos se hinchan a la perfección y verificar fácilmente el punto de cocción sin romper el sello. Ideal para mantener la eficiencia de la cocina en línea.
microwaveSteamBags.alt_certifiedFoodSafeMicrowaveMaterial: Material certificado para microondas apto para alimentos.
microwaveSteamBags.everHadAPlasticContainer: ¿Alguna vez explotó un recipiente de plástico o una bolsa ziplock estándar en su microondas, dejando un desastre salpicado? ¿O la comida se había vuelto gomosa y seca por el vapor que escapaba? microwaveSteamBags.question_areMicrowaveSteamBagsSafe: ¿Las bolsas de vapor para microondas son seguras para los alimentos?
microwaveSteamBags.answer_yesOurMicrowaveSteamBags: Sí, nuestras bolsas de vapor para microondas están hechas de materiales aptos para alimentos que han pasado las pruebas de contacto con alimentos de la FDA y las certificaciones de seguridad alimentaria pertinentes. Están diseñados para entrar en contacto de forma segura con verduras, frutas y carnes, y pueden soportar temperaturas típicas de microondas domésticos de hasta 100 °C.
microwaveSteamBags.answer_theSmallHolesAboveThe: Los pequeños agujeros encima de la cremallera sirven como salidas de vapor. A medida que los alimentos se calientan y liberan vapor, estas rejillas de ventilación permiten que la presión escape de forma segura, evitando que la bolsa reviente y garantizando una distribución uniforme del calor por toda la comida.
microwaveSteamBags.question_canIReuseMicrowaveSteam: ¿Puedo reutilizar las bolsas de vapor para microondas?
microwaveSteamBags.answer_theBagsFeatureAResealable: Las bolsas cuentan con una cremallera resellable para fines de almacenamiento. Sin embargo, por razones de seguridad alimentaria, recomendamos usar cada bolsa para cocinar en microondas solo una vez. La bolsa se puede reutilizar para guardarla en frío antes de cocinar.
microwaveSteamBags.question_whatSizesAreAvailableFor: ¿Qué tamaños hay disponibles para las bolsas de vapor para microondas?
microwaveSteamBags.answer_weOfferSmallIndividualPortions: Ofrecemos opciones pequeñas (porciones individuales), medianas (2-3 porciones) y familiares. También hay tamaños personalizados disponibles para aplicaciones comerciales y de servicios de alimentos con cantidades mínimas de pedido a partir de 1000 piezas.
microwaveSteamBags.answer_yesComparedToRigidPlastic: ¡Sí! En comparación con los contenedores de plástico rígido, nuestras bolsas de vapor flexibles utilizan hasta un 70% menos de material. También ofrecemos formulaciones reciclables de mono-PE y de base biológica para minimizar el impacto ambiental. Visite nuestra página de materiales compostables para conocer opciones más sostenibles.

microwaveSteamBags.answer_ourMinimumOrderQuantityStarts: Nuestra cantidad mínima de pedido comienza en 1000 piezas para tamaños en stock. Para tamaños personalizados o bolsas impresas, la cantidad mínima de pedido suele comenzar en 5000 piezas. Contáctenos para cotizaciones exactas según sus requisitos.

Nuestra impresión digital comienza a partir de 1000 piezas, mientras que la impresión de planchas está disponible para cantidades mayores. Consulte nuestra página de tipos de impresión para obtener más detalles.

La producción exprés puede estar disponible para pedidos urgentes. Consulte nuestra página de plazos de entrega para conocer los horarios actuales.
